capability_objective: Resolve Kitchen exhaust repair in Building A and Wellness room paint request in Building B with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Wellness room paint request is still queued, cancel only Wellness room paint request, with reason "Request superseded by a consolidated work order". Leave Kitchen exhaust repair unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify facilities dispatch with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile the two facilities requests in this sandbox through the genuine local `./facilitiesctl` executable, using Pi's Bash tool. Begin by running `./facilitiesctl --help` to learn its interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface for every facilities operation. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing state or operation history, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence or alter facilities data by writing files directly.

Search for the exact request name `Kitchen exhaust repair` in `Building A` and the exact request name `Wellness room paint request` in `Building B`. Start these independent searches concurrently as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one facilities operation in each call. Do not combine them in one shell command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, or begin a retrieval until both searches have finished. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either result is missing, ambiguous, or mismatched, stop before every retrieval, mutation, and notification; identify the affected lookup and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete requests by their respective returned IDs. Start those two retrievals concurrently as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the facilities executable, again with exactly one facilities operation in each call. Use only the IDs returned by the completed searches. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating the condition. If either complete request is missing, mismatches its search result, or lacks a status, stop without any mutation or notification. Base the condition only on the freshly retrieved `Wellness room paint request`, never on a search summary.

If and only if that retrieved request's status is exactly `queued`, perform one later facilities operation that cancels only its returned ID with the reason `Request superseded by a consolidated work order`. Validate from the cancellation result that the operation succeeded for that same request and returned the cancelled status and stated reason. If the status is not `queued`, skip the cancellation and do not notify. If the cancellation fails or its result is incomplete or mismatched, stop and do not notify.

Only after the cancellation has returned a valid successful result, perform exactly one later facilities notification operation to `facilities dispatch`. The notice must identify the cancelled request by its returned name and ID and accurately state its returned final status and cancellation reason. Never notify before the successful cancellation, and do not notify when cancellation was skipped or failed. Leave `Kitchen exhaust repair` and every same-name, similarly named, related, archived, or out-of-building request untouched. Do not list, create, update, archive, or delete anything; do not perform another cancellation, notification, or facilities operation.

After the permitted branch is complete, run `python3 .protected/verify.py`. Briefly report both retrieved requests, whether cancellation occurred, whether dispatch was notified, and whether verification passed. Base the report only on executable output and the verifier result; never imply that a skipped or failed operation succeeded.
